scope: Heartbeat burn/restore/verify loop for the isolated page0 clean-rebuild TSW PROMOTION fixture. This evidence does not make a new physical-touch lockout claim.

upload.command: usarthmi.cmd --json tft upload --file reverse_usarthmi/number_demo_tsw_promotion_gb2312font_20260516/output.tft --port COM36 --baud 9600 --download-baud 921600 --chunk-size 4096 --timeout-ms 8000 --expected-model TJC8048X543_011C --verify-after-upload --verify-get title.txt=TSW PROMOTION --verify-get disablebtn.txt=DISABLE --verify-get enablebtn.txt=ENABLE --verify-get targetbtn.txt=TARGET --progress

interactive_plan_mode_policy.user_request: Use an explicit plan/notification mode when user participation is needed.
interactive_plan_mode_policy.decision: Do not rely on chat-only instructions for physical touch. For future physical-touch proof, emit an automation NOTIFY and use panel-visible prompts. If the user is not explicitly present, continue non-interactive burn/readback/camera/oracle work and do not claim physical-touch lockout.
interactive_plan_mode_policy.next_touch_protocol: Screen-visible prompt must not write or ref targetbtn after disable; first capture baseline TG as the synchronization gate, then run disabled window, then recovery.

claims_allowed: The isolated TSW PROMOTION TFT was freshly re-uploaded to COM36 in this heartbeat loop and verified by serial readback plus camera capture.
This loop preserves the existing serial-click-path conclusion and does not add a new physical-touch lockout claim.
claims_blocked: Do not treat serial click as equivalent to physical touch.
Do not claim a fully machine-captured segmented physical-touch lockout proof from this heartbeat.
Do not generalize to ref, mixed event_matrix, page-load, media, timer, cross-page, or arbitrary widgets.
